compatibilityLabel: React DOM 19.2.6 createRoot container validation and root marker oracle

generation.method: checked runtime inventory plus exact npm tarballs extracted into a temporary node_modules tree and probed through a deterministic minimal DOM host
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per React DOM container root marker mode
generation.pathNormalization: temporary roots, package roots, workspace paths, and randomized React container marker suffixes are normalized before artifact serialization

probeModes.reason: Default Node development probes createRoot validation, marker cleanup, and duplicate-root diagnostics with development warnings enabled.
Default Node production probes createRoot validation and marker cleanup without development-only diagnostics.

validContainerCases.reason: Element nodes are valid createRoot containers.
Document nodes are valid createRoot containers.
DocumentFragment nodes are valid createRoot containers.

invalidContainerCases.reason: Null is rejected before any root marker side effect.
Undefined is rejected before any root marker side effect.
Plain objects without a valid DOM nodeType are rejected.
Text nodes are not valid createRoot containers.
The stable 19.2.6 package rejects comment mount-point containers because comments-as-DOM-containers are disabled.
Non-mount-point comments are rejected createRoot containers.

records: appendChild, insertBefore, removeChild, and textContent writes
createRoot root marker property counts with randomized suffixes removed
limitations: no browser parser, layout, focus, selection, accessibility, or custom element lifecycle behavior
delegated listener installation details are intentionally left to the root-listener oracle track
root.render DOM commits, hydration, resources, and event dispatch are outside this oracle

args.value: You are calling ReactDOMClient.createRoot() on a container that has already been passed to createRoot() before. Instead, call root.render() on the existing root instead if you want to update it.
You are calling ReactDOMClient.createRoot() on a container that was previously passed to ReactDOM.render(). This is not supported.

intentionalGaps.reason: This oracle records pinned React DOM 19.2.6 behavior only; Fast React DOM root implementation work is outside this worker scope.

The probe host implements deterministic DOM APIs needed for createRoot validation, marker writes, unmount cleanup, and listener installation side effects. Browser layout, parser, focus, selection, custom elements, and accessibility behavior are outside this oracle.
createRoot necessarily installs delegated listeners, but exact listener registration behavior belongs to the separate root-listener oracle track.
The oracle probes createRoot before any root.render call and root.unmount after an empty root only; DOM commits, hydration, resources, forms, and event dispatch are separate React DOM surfaces.
